stimulus: If an animal can use simple objects in its environment to accomplish a goal ("the tool-use test"), it is considered to display cognitive flexibility. In a recent experiment, researchers ______ for evidence of cognitive flexibility in octopuses, marine invertebrates that rely on a remarkably decentralized nervous system, adapted the test for underwater conditions, using clear plastic cylinders sealed with screw-on lids to challenge giant Pacific octopuses and common European octopuses.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The convention being tested is the use of verb forms within a sentence. The nonfinite present participle "looking" correctly forms a supplementary element that modifies the subject "researchers," indicating that the researchers who adapted the test were searching for evidence of cognitive flexibility in octopuses.

Choice A is incorrect because the finite present perfect verb "have looked" cannot be used in this way to form a supplementary modifying element. Choice B is incorrect because the finite past tense verb "looked" cannot be used in this way to form a supplementary modifying element. Choice C is incorrect because the finite past progressive verb "were looking" cannot be used in this way to form a supplementary modifying element.
